L1.area: Data exchange and inter-process communication in the distributed inference pipeline

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where untrusted data received over network sockets is deserialized using Python's pickle module without validation or authentication. A remote unauthenticated attacker can send crafted serialized objects to execute arbitrary code on the server.

L3.area: Encoder-decoder disaggregation communication layer in the distributed serving runtime
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists in the encoder parallel disaggregation system where embedding data received over ZMQ sockets is deserialized using Python's pickle module without validation or authentication. A remote unauthenticated attacker can send crafted serialized payloads to achieve arbitrary code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The disaggregation module deserializes untrusted data received over ZMQ network sockets using pickle.loads() without any authentication or input validation, enabling unauthenticated remote code execution.
